title: Some questions observations about the committee
display_title: Some questions observations about the committee
wikitext:
[]: Some questions/observations about the committee             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:09 pm If this has already been brought up then please delete this thread. I couldn't find anything with the search function. This is from episode two: (I apologise from the bad picture quality in all my 'screencaps' I'm simply using a camera). What caught my attention is that they are sitting inside an ATF (perhaps Nerv mastered how to communicate via ATFs, it would explain the strange location, as well as people suddenly 'teleporting' out of the room. Or maybe it is simply a glyph of one). Also, the reoccurring multicolour theme (four of the committee) we see this in the Angel's gene sequence shot, one of the earlier Seele crests as well as when Rei is being scanned for a snug fit into her new plugsuit. I will post these screencaps in a few days unless someone is feeling kind enough to do it for me. They are also sitting in three columns. Just like the Tree of Life (see below). Perhaps they are forming the top six sephiroth by accident? :P What does this all mean? Itâ€™s obviously connecting Seele to the Angels, but whenever I think this it feels like something essential is being left out, that Iâ€™m missing something. What do you think?

[]: Re: Some questions/observations about the committee             Quote Post by Space Penis » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:22 pm Zugzwang wrote: What caught my attention is that they are sitting inside an ATF (perhaps Nerv mastered how to communicate via ATFs, it would explain the strange location, as well as people suddenly 'teleporting' out of the room. Or maybe it is simply a glyph of one).

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:25 pm eeeeer......I think that they are actually 'sitting' (they're most propably holograms) in one table This is interesting though, I want to see the other pics

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:47 pm I heart the commitee, but I don't think there's anything special about how they are sitting, four guys, a grouchy cyborg and Gendo sitting at a table probably doesn't represent anything. There are only so many ways you can set around a rectangular table. The only thing I can discern from it is that one is Russian (Blue), one is a Frenchie (comical looking yellow guy), one is an American (green, Teddy Roosevelt wannabe), and one is a Brit (Red). Those are the nations that made up the Big Four in WWII. But if anyone wants more pics of them my avatar is one and I'll post some others.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:49 pm This doesn't seem like a very solid connection. The numbers just don't add up. Remember once again, that the kabbalistic references in the show only carry the most general of connotations and are primarily for dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 13, 2006 4:52 pm Those lights aren't ATF they are simply pretty colored lights that serve no freakin' purpose beyond trying to look cool. It comes off as a bit weird if you ask me. They don't teleport either, they are just holograms, like the monoliths (which are NOT computers).

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jan 13, 2006 7:07 pm Man those are just some ugly ugly people . . . .

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 13, 2006 8:16 pm They are not ugly! Especially the Russian he's so ugly he's cute. He's like four foot ten inches, how cute is that! If I wasn't already a Keel fangirl I'd be a commitee fangirl. Except for the Frenchie....he has hair (and a face to an extent) like a women. Plus the poor guy's nose, hasn't he ever heard of rhinoplasty? Over at the AnimeNation forums we've been working on developin fanon names for these guys. If your really bored sometime just clicky! http://animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=167003&page=29&pp=15

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jan 13, 2006 8:31 pm Keel has fangirls? :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Jan 13, 2006 8:36 pm Yup, I love that cranky old cyborg. To the point that Reichu has dubbed me the preistess of Keel.

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Sat Jan 14, 2006 9:43 am Except for the Frenchie....he has hair (and a face to an extent) like a women.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Jan 14, 2006 9:50 am I've thought he looks like her for a long time too. They have practily the same hair, add bangs to his hair and you have Ritsuko's hair, he has the face shape of a girl so just resize the nose and he's practily Ritsuko. Poor guy....he probably had miserable high school years.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jan 14, 2006 10:49 am That french dude just looks like a gargoyle.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 17, 2006 2:12 pm Tomorrow I will post the screenshots, but for now: Gendo and Keel are bathed in white light. As is Adam. The four other members of the committee are bathed in multi-toned light. Adam is the source, the divine light that the other Angels are 'seperated' from (who we will see also have multi-tonal genes). Adam diffuses his light into various threads, like a prism, the Angels combined make up Adamâ€™s soul-light (or lightbody) there is a hierarchy, and the same hierarchy is expressed visually within the committee (Gendo and Keel = Lilith and Adam?). The top six sephiroth fit into the germ of life/flower of life exactly. At the centre of the table then would be Daath, or chaos/knowledge - and some have theorised, Lucifer. Lucifer = Dead Sea Scrolls? Now, I havenâ€™t done this yet but itâ€™s common to overlay the Tarot with the Tree of Life, maybe the cards will correspond to each of the members (As in, the colour they are assigned as well as their physical/psychological characteristics). This system ties in to what I said in the Dirac (64) thread (metacosmic void = the white source). Plus, the committee works in a similar way to the Magi system (the three columns) on the left and right are yin yang/black and white, while within the middle column (balanced) the decisions are made (I forget which thread I brought this up, but it was recently. Edit: Here ). Diffused power expressed in a visual format. One more thing, The Eva Monkey, if your reading this - is Aaron your real name? Or is it one you simply use online because of what it means?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Tue Jan 17, 2006 5:09 pm Gendo and Keel are bathed in white light. As is Adam

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jan 18, 2006 12:47 pm The Seele glyph can be found here. The colour quality is poor, but the technicolour scheme that is used thoughtout the show, I think, is used when a scene is connected to the Angels/Adam and Lilith in some way.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Jan 18, 2006 3:12 pm Or it just looks cool? They do do that in anime, make things cool for coolness sake.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Jan 18, 2006 3:41 pm For once I'm agreeing with DD.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Wed Jan 18, 2006 4:45 pm Its a rainbow, I've always taken the SEELE symbol as one of two things. The seven-eyed sign of god, with the rainbow as a symbol for all of humanity. OR. What else could the inverted triangle and rainbow mean but Gay Pride? Seriously does this man not look gay? The plucked eyebrows? The overly scuplted hairdo? And check out this guy err lady. A transexual if I ever saw one! For heaven's sake he has Ritsuko's hair cut without the bangs. :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Jan 20, 2006 9:44 am DatDude wrote: Or it just looks cool? They do do that in anime, make things cool for coolness sake.
